behaviors.description: A file created in a nested subdirectory under the watched root is processed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at watcher->event->pipeline boundary for recursive paths, not a single function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: recursive watch behavior depends on real OS watcher semantics; mocked watcher can falsely pass. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if implementation explicitly walks directories on a polling loop, Integration could be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST: System wins because recursive event delivery differs by OS/backend; flip to Integration if behavior text guaranteed polling/in-process synthetic event source only. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt`, assert exactly one transform output appears for that file without restart.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses real filesystem events, matching System boundary.

behaviors.description: Deduplication drops a second request for the same path within a configured window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is core dedupe algorithm/state logic (path key + time window), not inherently module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if dedupe correctness depends on path normalization from another module, Integration may be required. TOP2-CONTRAST: Unit wins because behavior text is same file path + time window logic; flip to Integration if requirement adds cross-module canonicalization or queue side effects. Rejected higher levels: text does not require real FS events ('processing request' can be injected directly).
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedupe component with fake clock; submit same normalized path twice within window and once after window; assert accept/reject decisions and expiry behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Pure in-process state/time logic, so Unit is sufficient.

behaviors.description: Multiple OS filesystem events for one write still result in exactly one processing run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when real watcher emits bursty duplicate events and daemon coalescing interacts with runtime timing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked event streams usually miss backend-specific duplicate patterns. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if dedupe is entirely event-idempotency logic already validated with representative synthetic bursts, Integration might catch it. TOP2-CONTRAST: System wins because trigger condition explicitly references OS-emitted duplicates; flip to Integration if project uses a deterministic polling abstraction independent of OS events. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: On real filesystem, perform write pattern known to emit multiple events (create+append+rename/close) and verify pipeline side effect occurs once for that path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real OS event behavior, so lower levels are insufficient.

behaviors.description: If watched directory is deleted and recreated, daemon resumes watching without restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at long-lived watcher handle lifecycle across real directory inode removal/recreation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: only real OS watcher/inode behavior exposes this reliably. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if watcher adapter already abstracts delete/recreate as explicit callbacks, Integration may suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST: System wins because remove/recreate semantics are OS primitive behavior; flip to Integration if adapter contract is fully deterministic and independently system-tested. Rejected higher levels: behavior text excludes AI/ML and agent pipeline steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory, delete watched dir, recreate same path, then add new file; assert daemon processes post-recreation file without process restart.

behaviors.description: Simultaneous arrivals are processed in creation-timestamp order.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering depends on interaction of fs metadata retrieval, event race timing, and queue scheduler. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic events can hide timestamp resolution and ordering edge cases seen on real filesystems. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if ordering module receives explicit timestamps from caller, Integration could prove comparator/queue logic. TOP2-CONTRAST: System wins because requirement anchors to actual file creation timestamps under concurrent arrivals; flip to Integration if timestamp acquisition is out of scope and already trusted. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision content in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files near-simultaneously on real fs with controlled creation times, run daemon, and assert output processing order matches observed creation timestamps.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers real metadata and concurrency behavior that mocks may mask.

behaviors.description: A file still being written is deferred until write completion before processing.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure manifests from real write-in-progress timing (partial content, open handles, close events) across OS/fs semantics. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes often cannot reproduce true write completion signals and race windows. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if daemon uses deterministic 'stable-size for N ms' policy, Integration with a controllable fake clock/filesystem could catch many defects. TOP2-CONTRAST: System wins because correctness hinges on real write lifecycle and event timing; flip to Integration if policy is purely timer-based and fs behavior is abstracted behind a proven adapter. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML/model-driven correctness in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Begin streaming a large file in chunks while daemon runs; assert no processing before final chunk/close, then assert single processing after completion with full content.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real filesystem write timing to expose partial-read races.
